title: Cheruvathur Mahadeva Temple
tagline: Cheruvathur Mahadeva Temple — spiritual town of Kerala

overview.short: Cheruvathur Sri Mahadeva Temple, located in Kunnamkulam town of Thrissur district of Kerala.
overview.description: Cheruvathur Sri Mahadeva Temple, located in Kunnamkulam town of Thrissur district of Kerala. The presiding deity of the temple is Shiva, located in main sanctum sanatorium facing east. According to folklore, sage Parasurama has installed the idol. The temple is a part of the 108 famous Shiva temples in Kerala.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer
Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Chovvanur burial cave
topPlaces.description: Chovvanur Burial Cave is a prehistoric Megalith rock cut cave situated in Chowannur, of Thrissur District of Kerala. The cave can be accessed from single entry and its chamber is circular.

topPlaces.name: Kunnamkulam
topPlaces.description: Kunnamkulam Malayalam: [kun̪ːəm kuɭ̺ːəm] is a municipal town located in the Thrissur District of Kerala, India, with historical origins tracing back to the Paleolithic age. Its strategic location and excellent connectivity make it an important economic and cultural node, linking the southern regions of Kerala with North Malabar, and serving as a crucial point for travel and trade.

topPlaces.name: Chowannur
topPlaces.description: Chowannur is a place in Thrissur district, state of Kerala, India.

topPlaces.name: St. Thomas Forane Church, Mattom
topPlaces.description: St. Thomas Forane Church is a Syro-Malabar Catholic church located in Mattom of Thalapilly Taluk in the Thrissur district of Kerala, south India.

topPlaces.name: Chiravarampathukavu Bhagavathi Temple
topPlaces.description: Chiravarampathukavu Temple enshrines Bhagawati, the mother Goddess, one of the most popular deities in Kerala. The town of Aruvayi, Pazhanji is near the city of Kunnamkulam.

topPlaces.name: Kadavallur
topPlaces.description: Kadavallur is a village in the north of Thrissur district in Kerala, India. This village is the border of Thrissur and Malappuram districts, and is also very close to Palakkad district.

topPlaces.name: St. Mary's Orthodox Cathedral, Arthat
topPlaces.description: Arthat St. Mary's Orthodox Cathedral (Arthat Valiyapally), also called Kunnamkulam-Chattukulangara Church, is an ancient Oriental Orthodox church located in Arthat village of Thalapilly taluk, one mile south of Kunnamkulam town, in Thrissur district, Kerala, India.

topPlaces.name: Chemmanthatta
topPlaces.description: Chemmanthatta is a village located in the Thrissur district of Kerala, India, approximately 6 kilometers from Kunnamkulam town. A notable landmark in Chemmanthatta is the Chemmanthatta Mahadeva Temple, a Hindu temple dedicated to Lord Shiva.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Cheruvathur Mahadeva Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Cheruvathur Mahadeva Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Cheruvathur Mahadeva Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Calicut International Airport, Kozhikode (55 km) and the nearest railway station is Ernakulam Junction (Kochi) (80 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Cheruvathur Mahadeva Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Cheruvathur Mahadeva Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Cheruvathur Mahadeva Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Cheruvathur Mahadeva Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Cheruvathur Mahadeva Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Chovvanur burial cave, Kunnamkulam, Chowannur, St. Thomas Forane Church, Mattom, Chiravarampathukavu Bhagavathi Temple.
